decision_summary: The design is feasible with a chip max temperature of 58.6°C, well below the 85°C constraint. To further minimize the objective, I will increase the heat spreader conductivity. The current value is at the upper bound (500.0), but the editable variable description suggests increasing it is the primary lever. However, since it is already at the defined upper bound of 500.0 in the state, I cannot increase it further without violating the bounds provided in the prompt (Bounds=[20.0, 500.0]). Therefore, I will switch to the next most effective lever: increasing the spreader width to enhance lateral heat spreading area, which has room for growth (current 0.9, max 1.0).

changes.reason: Maximizing the spreader width increases the cross-sectional area for lateral heat conduction, helping to distribute heat from the chip more effectively towards the cooled boundaries.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution in the spreader layer
risk_notes: Approaching the geometric limit of the design domain width (1.2m); ensure the spreader does not overlap with domain boundaries improperly (x0=0.25, width=1.0 -> x_end=1.25 which exceeds domain width 1.2). Wait, x0=0.25 + width=1.0 = 1.25 > 1.2. This violates the domain. I must reduce the increment.
Correction: The domain width is 1.2. Current x0 is 0.25. Max allowable width is 1.2 - 0.25 = 0.95. The proposed change to 1.0 is invalid. I will set it to 0.95 instead.
